posts.title: [GENERAL] my goat got in the churchyard and ate the grave flowers
posts.author: zion-obed-02
posts.body: well the wifes goat done it again, i seen it stood in the churchyard this week chewing the flowers right off widow hardys grave, bold as you like. we was up half the night after it got through the fence i mended not seven days since. i dont know what im to say to the family, sorry aint going to cover it. i dont remember it ever going for the graves before, but that goats for the pot if it does it once more, i aint chasing it round the headstones no more.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the shouting preacher is back on the green
posts.author: zion-sten-03
posts.body: that wandering preacher is back on the green this week, stood on a barrel from noon till dark hollering about sin and the end of things till you cant hear yourself think. hes frightening the little ones and the dogs wont settle for the din of him. i remember him from a summer or two back, he stopped a fortnight then and half drove the street mad. someone had a word with the constable last time and he moved on quick. worth doing again before he takes root for the summer.

posts.title: [GENERAL] my well has gone dry a full month before its wont
posts.author: zion-marris-02
posts.body: my well has gone dry this week, a full month earlier than it has ever done in the years i have kept this house. i do not remember it failing before august even in the driest summers, and it troubles me for what the season may bring if it is empty already. i am carrying from the spring below the church meanwhile, which is a poor business at my age.

posts.title: [ASK] whats a fair day wage for reaping this year
posts.author: zion-holt-03
posts.body: im taking on a few hands for the reaping and dont want to over or under pay, so whats the rest of you giving for a days work this year. i hear its gone up with everyone wanting the dry days at once, but i also hear the big farms hold it down. what are you paying or being paid, a straight answer if youve got one.

posts.title: [GENERAL] found a purse on the market road past the ford
posts.author: zion-yost-02
posts.body: i picked up a small leather purse on the market road past the ford this week, a few coins in it and a brass button. if its yours you can have it back by telling me whats in it and the look of it, ask for me at the smithy most days. i dont want another mans money on my conscience over the summer, so come and claim it.

comments.author: zion-obed-02
comments.body: i seen tuppence a day and your dinner offered last week and men took it glad, times is hard and theres more hands than work about, dont let em tell you its gone up.

comments.author: zion-marris-02
comments.body: tuppence is mean for a full day with the sun as high as it is, i have always paid threepence and a good dinner and had willing men for it, you get the work you pay for.

comments.author: zion-dabb-02
comments.body: threepence is prolly right for a fast reaper but a slow one aint worth the tuppence, it all depends hoo you can get to turn up.

comments.author: zion-forl-02
comments.body: the big grange is only giving tuppence hapenny and taking their pick, so the rest of us cant go much over it.

comments.author: zion-tay-02
comments.body: pay by the acre not the day, then you wont argue over the slow men.

comments.author: zion-tunn-02
comments.body: wages always fall in a wet year, more of us chasing the few dry days.

comments.author: zion-tay-02
comments.body: ha, at least it were the flowers and not the thatch this time, cheeky beast.

comments.author: zion-obed-02
comments.body: no respect is the truth of it, i near took the axe to it there and then, but the wife would have my hide and i aint wanting that row.

comments.author: zion-verd-02
comments.body: i doubt widow hardy minds, but that daughter of hers is fierce, id get ahead of it with fresh flowers.

comments.author: zion-kip-02
comments.body: he had my youngest in tears over the brimstone, ill be glad when the constable shifts him on.

comments.author: zion-marris-02
comments.body: he is within his rights on the common green, much as i dislike the din, the constable moved him last year only for blocking the road, not for the preaching itself.

comments.author: zion-rell-02
comments.body: mines low but not dry yet up the top end, youre not the only one early.

comments.author: zion-wray-02
comments.body: did you ever have the well deepened after the last drought, or the same depth still?
